top_stories.title: Microsoft Patches Entra ID Role Flaw That Enabled Service Principal Takeover
top_stories.summary: Microsoft patched a flaw in the Entra ID "Agent ID Administrator" role that could allow privilege escalation and service principal takeover attacks. Silverfort researchers discovered the vulnerability in the built-in role, which was introduced to manage AI agent identity lifecycle operations within Microsoft's agent identity platform.

top_stories.title: Microsoft fixes Entra ID flaw enabling privilege escalation
top_stories.summary: Microsoft patched a privilege escalation vulnerability in Microsoft Entra ID that allowed users with the Agent ID Administrator role to perform account takeover of service accounts. The flaw could be exploited to escalate privileges within the Entra ID identity platform.

top_stories.title: cPanel zero-day exploited for months before patch release (CVE-2026-41940)
top_stories.summary: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-41940) in cPanel has been actively exploited in the wild since at least February 23, months before a patch was released. The zero-day affects cPanel's web-based hosting control panel and allows attackers to bypass authentication controls.

top_stories.title: Cybercrime Groups Using Vishing and SSO Abuse in Rapid SaaS Extortion Attacks
top_stories.summary: Two cybercrime groups, Cordial Spider and Snarky Spider, are conducting rapid SaaS extortion attacks that abuse SSO systems alongside vishing tactics to gain unauthorized access. The attacks operate almost entirely within SaaS environments while leaving minimal forensic traces, with threat actors leveraging SSO abuse as a primary intrusion vector.

top_stories.title: Learning from the Vercel breach: Shadow AI & OAuth sprawl
top_stories.summary: The Vercel breach demonstrated how a compromised third-party OAuth integration can create widespread access risks across downstream customers. The incident highlights the risks of OAuth sprawl and shadow AI integrations as attack vectors in modern identity security.

news.title: Identity is the control plane for distributed infrastructure
news.summary: Teleport CEO Ev Kontsevoy argues that distributed infrastructure spanning cloud, Kubernetes, databases, and servers requires a unified identity layer rather than additional tools layered on fragmented identity systems. He advocates for fewer credentials, fewer entry points, and consolidated identity control for security and engineering teams.

news.title: Poisoned Ruby Gems and Go Modules Exploit CI Pipelines for Credential Theft
news.summary: A software supply chain attack campaign attributed to the GitHub account "BufferZoneCorp" is using malicious Ruby gems and Go modules to steal credentials and tamper with CI/CD pipelines. The attack employs sleeper packages that later push payloads enabling credential theft, GitHub Actions manipulation, and SSH persistence.

news.title: Nine-year-old Linux kernel flaw enables reliable local privilege escalation (CVE-2026-31431)
news.summary: Security researchers at Theori disclosed a high-severity local privilege escalation vulnerability (CVE-2026-31431) in the Linux kernel, present in virtually all major Linux distributions since 2017. A working proof-of-concept exploit is publicly available for the flaw, which allows local attackers to escalate privileges on affected systems.

news.title: Govern your bots carefully or chaos could ensue
news.summary: Gartner warns that AI agent sprawl poses significant governance risks as Global Fortune 500 enterprises are expected to run over 150,000 AI agents by 2028, up from fewer than 15 today. The article addresses the need for proper identity governance and access controls applied to non-human identities and autonomous AI agents.

news.title: SAP-Related npm Packages Compromised in Credential-Stealing Supply Chain Attack
news.summary: Cybersecurity researchers from multiple firms uncovered a supply chain attack campaign called "Mini Shai-Hulud" targeting SAP-related npm packages with credential-stealing malware. The attack compromises npm packages to steal credentials, impacting organizations using SAP-associated JavaScript libraries.

news.title: Webinar: How to Automate Exposure Validation to Match the Speed of AI Attacks
news.summary: A webinar is being promoted around the threat of AI-automated attacks targeting Active Directory and Domain Admin credentials. The content focuses on autonomous agents capable of mapping Active Directory and escalating privileges rapidly, framed around exposure validation defensive workflows.

news.title: Critical cPanel Authentication Vulnerability Identified — Update Your Server Immediately
news.summary: cPanel has released security updates to address a critical authentication vulnerability affecting all supported versions of cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM). The flaw impacts various authentication paths and could allow attackers to gain unauthorized access to the control panel software.

news.title: Organizing Agents’ memory at scale: Namespace design patterns in AgentCore Memory
news.summary: AWS published a technical post on AgentCore Memory covering namespace design patterns, retrieval methods, and IAM-based access control implementation. The IAM component is a supporting feature within a broader AI agent memory architecture discussion, not the primary focus of the article.

news.title: AWS Transfer Family Terraform module now supports Okta and Microsoft Entra ID integration examples
news.summary: AWS Transfer Family's Terraform module has been updated to include end-to-end deployment examples integrating Okta and Microsoft Entra ID as custom identity providers for authentication and access control. The update enables enterprises using these IdPs to authenticate and control access to AWS Transfer Family endpoints using their existing identity infrastructure.

news.title: Why Secure Data Movement Is the Zero Trust Bottleneck Nobody Talks About
news.summary: New research from an unnamed team surveyed 500 security professionals to examine how secure data movement creates bottlenecks in Zero Trust program implementation. The report, titled "Cyber360: Defending the Digital Battlespace," argues that assumptions about connectivity undermine Zero Trust deployments.

news.title: Access control with IAM Identity Center session tags
news.summary: AWS published guidance on using IAM Identity Center session tags for access control across multi-account environments. The article covers how session tags enable scalable, attribute-based access control (ABAC) for workforce identities managed through AWS IAM Identity Center.

news.title: Why AI agents are triggering a rethink of enterprise identity
news.summary: Computer Weekly's Security Think Tank examines how agentic AI is impacting enterprise identity and access management practices. The article focuses on the specific security stack implications of AI agents, which operate autonomously and require new approaches to identity, authentication, and authorization.

news.title: Silverfort acquires Fabrix Security to bring AI decision-making to runtime access control
news.summary: Silverfort has acquired Fabrix Security, an AI-native identity security startup founded in 2024, for an undisclosed sum. The acquisition is aimed at integrating AI-driven decision-making into runtime access control within Silverfort's unified identity security platform.

news.title: Simplifying AWS defense with Microsoft Sentinel UEBA
news.summary: Microsoft Sentinel's User and Entity Behavior Analytics (UEBA) capability is being used to analyze AWS CloudTrail logs for anomalous identity-based activity. The solution enriches raw log data with behavioral signals derived from baseline user, peer, and device behavior patterns to distinguish legitimate from malicious access.

news.title: Five-minute interview: Zair Abbas, Okta
news.summary: Okta's partner ecosystem lead for EMEA, Zair Abbas, participated in a brief personal insights interview. The article is a short profile piece focused on personal background rather than substantive IAM product or business developments.

news.title: How to fix cybersecurity's agentic AI identity crisis
news.summary: The article addresses security challenges posed by AI agents in enterprise environments, focusing on identity and access management for autonomous AI systems. The piece covers how agentic AI creates new requirements for authentication, authorization, and access control frameworks.

news.title: Can I do that with policy? Understanding the AWS Service Authorization Reference
news.summary: AWS published guidance on understanding the AWS Service Authorization Reference for IAM policy capabilities. The article covers what AWS IAM policies can control, including AWS Organizations service control policies and related policy mechanisms.

news.title: Why AI is forcing a reset of the identity stack
news.summary: Computer Weekly's Security Think Tank examines how AI is driving changes in identity and access management practices. The article focuses on the concept of "identity intelligence" as an evolution from traditional identity management approaches.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. He admitted to conducting SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in theft of tens of millions in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from users across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, instead silently siphoning authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access to Microsoft Office accounts.
